# Fractal Self-Similarity

## The Physics

The same structural patterns repeat at every scale of an organization. A team
of three follows the same governance rhythm as a division of three hundred:
define what things are, establish how they connect, decide who owns them,
and make them discoverable.

This is not a design choice — it is an observation about how effective
organizations work. When patterns DO repeat cleanly across scales, navigation
is intuitive and governance scales. When they DON'T, every level invents its
own conventions and knowledge fragments.

## Why It Matters

- **Navigation**: If you understand governance at one level, you understand it at every level.
- **Tooling**: Scanners, validators, and agents can use one set of rules everywhere.
- **Onboarding**: New participants learn one pattern, not N patterns for N scales.
- **Composition**: Small governed components compose into larger governed components without translation layers.

Without fractal self-similarity, governance becomes a bespoke exercise at each level,
creating cognitive overhead that grows with organizational scale.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Mandelbrot's fractal geometry: self-similar patterns in natural systems.
- Conway's Law: organizations design systems that mirror their communication structure.
- ISO/IEC 42010 (architecture frameworks): recursive decomposition of systems.
- Microservices architecture: the same interface contract pattern at service, team, and platform levels.

## The Fractal Check

When introducing any new component type, the fractal check asks: **at what
levels does this type apply?** JOS level, venture level, product level,
sub-component level. If a type applies at multiple levels, its schema must
support that from day one — retrofitting multi-level support is expensive.

This check is operationalized in JOS-SOP-006 (Adding a New Component Type)
as Step 1B. It applies the fractal self-similarity concept as a concrete
gate: you cannot create a new type without documenting its fractal scope.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P07 (Ambient Discoverability) — discoverable registries are a fractal pattern
- JOS-S02 (Universal Placement) — classification is fractal governance in action
- JOS-R01 (Classification Required) — the testable enforcement of this concept
- JOS-SOP-006 (Adding a New Component Type) — fractal check is Step 1B

### Framework Connection

Framework §1 (dual-axis classification) and §2 (lens model) formalize how fractal self-similarity manifests as holonic recursion — every node is simultaneously a whole and a part. §4 (universal level pattern) confirms that the same structural pattern repeats at L0 through L3 at every anchor point.
